Vishay; IEC 61000-4-5 surge protection at J4 SIG terminal before R2; standoff 3.3V above normal 2.0V loop max — will not conduct in normal operation; clamp voltage ~5.3V@10A versus SMAJ5.0CA's 9.2V — tighter protection for ADS1115 AIN abs-max chain; LCSC C2836497

Vishay; clamps cable transients on VLOOP terminal before they reach TPS61023; between U8 VOUT and J4/J5 VLOOP pin; LCSC C8057

NXP; IO1 to 1WIRE DATA (J6 pin 2); IO2 no-connect; VCC to +3V3; GND to GND; clamp voltage VCC+0.5V = 3.8V during ESD transient (sub-ns); ESP32-C6 GPIO7 abs max is 3.6V but this brief overshoot is standard practice for IEC 61000-4-2 level-4 GPIO protection — add 33Ω series resistor R32 between J6 DATA and the D12/1WIRE junction if strict abs-max compliance is required; capacitance 0.5pF per channel well below 100pF 1-Wire add limit; same part as D1 — consolidate on one tape reel; LCSC C2687116

Vishay; placed across J1 BAT+ and BAT- (system ground side of J1 — before D5 reverse-polarity FET); standoff 5.0V is above 4.2V LiPo full-charge so TVS does not conduct in steady state; clamp voltage 9.2V@10A protects downstream circuits including S-8261AAYFT (VCC abs max ~6.5V) against cable-induction surges; bidirectional required because both charge and discharge current flow through the terminals; LCSC C2836466

Vishay; placed at J12 SOLAR+ terminal to GND before D6 Schottky; first-stage absorber for cable-coupled ESD and lightning induction; standoff 7.0V is above 6.5V max MPPT operating voltage so TVS does not conduct in normal operation; works in tandem with D8 (SMAJ7.0A after D6 at CN3791 VIN) to form two-stage protection chain; same MPN as D8 — consolidate on shared tape reel; LCSC C78310
DNF in production; omit to reduce active current
